paper_title: Estimating the timescale-dependent uncertainty of paleoclimate records – a spectral approach. Part I: Theoretical concept

methods.description: This method defines the reconstruction uncertainty model for estimating timescale-dependent uncertainty in paleoclimate records. It involves assumptions and simplifications about the climate signal structure, archive formation, and measurement procedures to analytically treat the problem.

steps.description: Suppose a structure of the true climate signal, which the final uncertainty estimates will be based upon.
steps.searchable_summary: define structure of true climate signal for uncertainty estimation

steps.description: Make simplifying assumptions regarding the archive formation, concerning proxy seasonality, the climate-proxy relationship, the sediment accumulation rate, and the effects of bioturbation mixing.
steps.searchable_summary: apply assumptions on archive formation and proxy relationships

steps.description: Specify the archive sampling and measurement procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: define archive sampling and measurement procedures

steps.description: Define the reconstruction error as the difference between the obtained climate reconstruction and a suitable reference climate.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate reconstruction error from climate reconstruction and reference

steps.description: Define the reconstruction uncertainty in terms of the expected value of the squared reconstruction error.
steps.searchable_summary: compute reconstruction uncertainty from squared error
